schemaorg_recipe: {"@type":"Recipe","name":"DRIED MIRABELLE PLUM SKINS, A CANDY MADE FROM ROSEMARY","description":"I n the late summer, wild plums ripen all over the island during a very short season. These plums can be red or yellow, and they have larger pits and thicker skin than cultivated plums. We collected them and experimented, working our way toward a plum dessert. One trial had the plums stewing uncovered in a pot, and we found that the skins floating to the top were exposed to the air and became leathery, with a concentrated flavor. After drying those skins a touch more in front of the hearth, they were perfect.","recipeYield":"4","recipeIngredient":["16 Mirabelle plums (about 1 kg)","Stock syrup (page 256)","1 (10-inch/25 cm) square rosemary candy (page 257), broken into 1/2-inch/1 cm shards"],"recipeInstructions":[{"@type":"HowToStep","text":"Quarter 3 of the best-looking plums and discard the pits. extracted_ingredients: 1 (10-inch/25 cm) square rosemary candy (page 257), broken into 1/2-inch/1 cm shards
extracted_instructions: Quarter 3 of the best-looking plums and discard the pits. Carefully slice the skins from each quarter, leaving them with about 1/8 inch/3 mm of flesh. Cover and refrigerate the skins and place the scrap in a medium metal mixing bowl.
